name: core.openwop.flow
description: Spec-canonical flow-control primitives. 25 nodes covering branching, merging, iteration, aggregation, batching, sorting, deduplication, repetition, suspension, sub-workflow invocation, and Make-style error handlers. Pure or suspending — no external I/O. Zero npm deps. Engine >=1.0.0 <2.0.0.
author: OpenWOP Working Group

nodes.label: If
nodes.description: Two-branch conditional. Evaluates a simple expression `{path, op, value}` against the input and routes to `then` or `else`. Ops: eq, neq, lt, lte, gt, gte, in, contains, starts-with, ends-with, regex, exists, not-exists, truthy, falsy.

nodes.label: Switch
nodes.description: N-way value-table lookup. Maps an input value to one of N output labels via an exact-match table, with optional `default` label.

nodes.label: Router
nodes.description: Fan-out to labelled branches, each with its own predicate. Evaluates routes top-to-bottom. First-match-wins or all-match modes. Optional default route.

nodes.label: Filter
nodes.description: Predicate-based drop. Same predicate shape as `core.flow.if`. Items matching pass through; items failing are dropped (returns `passed: false`).

nodes.label: Merge
nodes.description: Combine N input arrays. Modes: `append` (concat in order), `combine-by-key` (join on a key path), `combine-by-position` (zip), `multiplex` (cartesian product), `choose-branch` (return first non-empty input), `sql-query` (SQL JOIN — requires host.sql per RFC 0018).

nodes.label: Iterator
nodes.description: Decomposes an array into one output bundle per element. Downstream nodes run per element. Output includes `index` and `total` for cursor-aware downstream logic.

nodes.label: Aggregate (Array)
nodes.description: Inverse of iterator: collapse N input bundles into one bundle whose payload is the array of values.

nodes.label: Aggregate (Numeric)
nodes.description: Reduce a numeric field across an array. Operations: sum, count, avg, min, max. NaN-skipping. Empty input returns `null`.

nodes.label: Aggregate (Text)
nodes.description: Concatenate a string field across an array with a separator. Optional per-element template substitution.

nodes.label: Aggregate (Table)
nodes.description: Materialize bundles into a 2D table: ordered `columns[]` configuration + `rows[]` output. Useful upstream of CSV emit or BI sinks.

nodes.label: Split In Batches
nodes.description: Chunk an array into batches of N. Returns the current batch + `done` flag + `remaining` count. Workflows loop on this node by feeding the `remaining` array back in.

nodes.label: Sort
nodes.description: Sort an array by one or more key paths. Stable. Direction per-key. Numeric, string, or natural compare.

nodes.label: Limit
nodes.description: Return the first N items of an array. Optional `offset` for skip-then-take. No-ops on shorter arrays.

nodes.label: Distinct
nodes.description: Drop duplicates by key path (or full-element JSON canonicalization when path absent). Preserves first-occurrence order.

nodes.label: Compare Datasets
nodes.description: Diff two arrays by primary-key path. Returns four arrays: `addedIn2`, `removedFrom1`, `changed` (with `before`/`after`), `unchanged`.

nodes.label: Repeater
nodes.description: Generate N bundles with an incrementing counter `i` (start, step, count). Source-style node — emits without an input.

nodes.label: No-op
nodes.description: Pass-through placeholder. Returns its input unchanged. Useful as a labelled join point in a graph.

nodes.label: Stop And Error
nodes.description: Aborts the workflow with a structured error envelope (code + message + details). Side-effecting: terminates the run.

nodes.label: Wait
nodes.description: Suspend the run. Modes: `duration` (seconds, ≤86400), `until` (ISO-8601 timestamp), `webhook` (external HTTP callback signed per webhooks.md). Resumes via the existing interrupt mechanism (interrupt.md).

nodes.label: Invoke Sub-Workflow
nodes.description: Synchronously invoke another workflow. Delegates to reserved `core.subWorkflow` typeId per node-packs.md. Inputs forwarded; sub-workflow outputs returned. Failures propagate.

nodes.label: Error Handler — Resume
nodes.description: When attached to an upstream node's error route, substitute a fallback bundle and continue. Make.com semantics.

nodes.label: Error Handler — Ignore
nodes.description: Silently drop the failed bundle and continue. Make.com semantics.

nodes.label: Error Handler — Break
nodes.description: Stop the run and mark it incomplete so it can be reprocessed (retry semantics owned by the host). Make.com semantics.

nodes.label: Error Handler — Commit
nodes.description: Stop the run and commit transactional side effects upstream (DB writes, data-store mutations). Hosts without transactional adapters treat as Break.

nodes.label: Error Handler — Rollback
nodes.description: Stop the run and request rollback of transactional side effects upstream. Non-transactional side effects (sent emails, posted webhooks) cannot be undone.

nodes.label: Conditional (alias)
nodes.description: Reserved alias of core.flow.if (per node-packs.md §Reserved Core typeIds). Same runtime behavior; preserves the reserved name for spec compliance.

nodes.label: Delay (alias)
nodes.description: Reserved alias of core.flow.wait. Same runtime behavior; preserves the reserved name for spec compliance.

nodes.label: Loop (alias)
nodes.description: Reserved alias of core.flow.iterator. Same runtime behavior; preserves the reserved name for spec compliance.

nodes.label: Parallel (alias)
nodes.description: Reserved alias of core.flow.router (fan-out). Same runtime behavior; preserves the reserved name for spec compliance.
